query: our exporter crashes opening the second project but the first one is fine — where do I even start
rationale: systematic debugging: establish a repro, step before the crash

query: I'm debugging a read access violation and the pointer's memory is all 0xdddddddd — what does that mean and what should I check next
rationale: freed-memory fill pattern at a mapped address = use-after-free

query: the packaging command crashes but it's slow — can you help me find a smaller way to trigger it
rationale: shrink to a minimal deterministic repro

query: this regression appeared sometime in the last 200 commits, how do I find which change broke it
rationale: git bisect on a deterministic repro

query: CI started failing on the auth integration test after yesterday's deploy — help me find the root cause
rationale: integration failure: reproduce, trace boundary, minimal fix

query: the API returns 500 only when the request body has an empty array — walk me through debugging it
rationale: edge-case defect: repro, classify, regression test, minimal fix

query: while debugging repeated buffer overwrites, what design would make the bad write fault instantly instead of corrupting neighbors
rationale: prevention-by-design for overwrite class during active debugging

query: I keep editing things at random trying to make the crash go away and it's not working
rationale: flailing vs hypothesis-driven debugging

query: this bug only shows up once an hour and I can't catch it in the debugger — how do I make it reproducible
rationale: intermittent: stress, determinism, circular log, record/replay

query: while debugging this crash, where should I add assertions so a bad precondition fails at the call site instead of corrupting state later
rationale: assertions/invariants as tripwires during active debugging

query: I'm debugging a memory leak — walk me through file/line allocation tags and a zero-on-shutdown counter to find it
rationale: allocation instrumentation for leak detection while chasing a bug

query: I'm debugging dangling-pointer crashes on shutdown — is skipping destroy a real fix or just hiding the root cause
rationale: prevention reflection: never-free hides bugs; proper teardown exposes them

query: guide me through strict red-green-refactor for this bug fix — write the failing test first
rationale: near-miss: strict TDD coaching -> test-driven-dev-guide

query: audit whether my bug fix actually satisfies all the acceptance criteria and run the verification commands
rationale: near-miss: post-implementation correctness audit -> implementation-auditor

query: review my diff for the crash fix before I merge
rationale: near-miss: structured diff review -> code-reviewer

query: execute step 3 of the implementation plan on this branch
rationale: near-miss: plan execution -> plan-executor

query: design the ownership convention for our parser buffers so there are no leaks or double-frees on error paths
rationale: near-miss: lifetime/ownership design -> cpp-memory-guide, not finding a bug

query: the update loop is too slow, profile it and tell me which cache misses to fix
rationale: near-miss: profiling for speed without a defect

query: I'm refactoring Mesh.cpp and need LLDB commands to check whether this vector iterator is still valid
rationale: near-miss: native debugger recipes while editing C++ -> cpp-coding native-debugging

query: I have a 100% repro of a use-after-free; walk me through forming a hypothesis and confirming it before I touch the code
rationale: hypothesis, one change, confirm before fixing

query: make our engine tick a deterministic function of its inputs so the same input replays the same crash every time
rationale: designing for determinism + replay for intermittent bugs

query: the build broke after upgrading webpack — narrow down which config change caused it
rationale: build/config regression: reproduce, bisect or differential compare

query: convert this AoS particle array to SoA so the hot loop is cache friendly
rationale: near-miss: data layout for performance without a defect
